content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (like ICM and RND for autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new training samples an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, aligning sample selection with the agent’s natural knowledge gaps to push 2024-2025 SOTA performance. This creates a self-reinforcing memory cycle where the agent’s inherent curiosity drives both what it learns and what it retains, rather than relying on hand-engineered importance metrics for memory pruning.
